purpose: Maximum independence: visual → factors → computation → complete equation; integrates M8 (visual to factors) + M9 (factors to product).
visual: Context Visualization: 9 bags with 5 items each. Equation Builder: `[___] × [___] = [___]` (all three slots empty). Methods C/D active. Tile palette: 5, 9, 10, 14, 45, 50, 90, 95.
guide: "Last one. Look at the bags and build the whole equation."
student_action: Equation Builder Methods C or D (all three slots)
on_correct: "9 times 5 equals 45. Ends in 5 — matches the 5s pattern. You built the complete equation."
remediation_note: Direct student to extract factors from visual: count bags (first factor = 9), count items per bag (second factor = 5). Then skip-count by 5s nine times.
design_note: Final interaction — maximum independence. Student constructs entire equation (both factors + product) from Context Visualization only. No verbal statement of factors, no pre-filled slots, no Products Strip. Per SME feedback: verbal description ("9 groups of 5") actually gives the factors away — requiring students to extract factor information from the visual is the harder task and tests the full skill chain: identify groups and group size from image → map to correct equation slots → compute product via skip-counting → complete equation. This integrates M8 skill (visual → factors) with M9 skill (factors → product), appropriate for a "maximum independence" closer. Values (9×5=45) at the higher end, requiring extended skip-counting. Guide says "Look at the bags" to direct attention but does NOT state the factors.
